items.title: Watch: Match of the Day
items.summary: Highlights of the day's four Premier League games, including Tottenham v Manchester City.

items.title: Sources: Liverpool reach $74M deal for Jacquet
items.summary: Liverpool have reached a pre-agreement with Rennes to sign defender Jérémy Jacquet in the summer, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Chelsea recall Sarr, send out Anselmino
items.summary: Chelsea will recall Mamadou Sarr from his loan at sister club Strasbourg and sent Aaron Anselmino to France in a swap deal before the end of the transfer window on Monday, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Saint-Maximin leaves América after kids 'abused'
items.summary: Former Newcastle winger Allan Saint-Maximin has left Mexican side Club America by mutual consent after claiming that his children were the victims of racist abuse.

items.title: Liverpool beat Chelsea to agree £60m summer deal for Rennes centre-back Jérémy Jacquet
items.summary: Centre-back to join club at end of the current season Crystal Palace agree £48m deal for striker Strand Larsen Liverpool have won the race for the Rennes centre‑back Jérémy Jacquet. Chelsea were pushing hard but the 20-year-old has decided on a summer move to Anfield. The package is reportedly £55m plus £5m in add-ons. The club had explored a deal to bring Lutsharel Geertruida to Anfield before deciding not to proceed with the move. The Netherlands international, who joined Sunderland on loan from RB Leipzig last summer, has made 17 appearances this season and appears likely to remain in the north-east. Arne Slot worked with the versatile defender, who can play across the backline and also as a defensive midfielder, while at Feyenoord with the pair winning the Eredivisie in 2023. Continue reading...

items.title: Samenvatting: PSV heeft geen kind aan zwalkend Feyenoord
items.summary: PSV heeft zondagmiddag met speels gemak gewonnen van Feyenoord. Na ruim een kwartier kon de rem erop bij de koploper van de Eredivisie, die de bezoekers de snelste 3-0 achterstand ooit had bezorgd. Het gat met de Rotterdammers, de nummer twee op de ranglijst, bedraagt nu zeventien punten. Bekijk hier de samenvatting.

items.title: Samenvatting: Ajax knoeit weer na comfortabele voorsprong tegen Excelsior
items.summary: Ajax heeft zondagmiddag dure punten laten liggen op bezoek bij Excelsior. Mika Godts zette de Amsterdammers op een comfortabele voorsprong, maar in de slotfase kwamen de Rotterdammers langszij. Bekijk hier de samenvatting.

items.title: Copy of Premier League title-race twist: Questions for Man...
items.summary: The momentum has shifted in the Premier League title race again as Man City wasted a two-goal lead at Tottenham. How did that happen?

items.title: Copy of Carrick's calm amid the chaos is exactly what Man ...
items.summary: Michael Carrick has three wins from three games in charge at Man United.

items.title: 'Liverpool telt zestig miljoen euro neer voor Frans toptalent'
items.summary: Liverpool lijkt een grote slag te slaan op de transfermarkt. The Reds hebben naar verluidt een akkoord bereikt met Stade Rennes over de komst van Jérémy Jacquet. De bedoeling is dat de twintigjarige verdediger in de zomer naar Anfield verkast.

items.title: European football: Mbappé wins it late for Real Madrid after Bellingham limps off
items.summary: Bellingham out for month with hamstring injury PSG return to top of Ligue 1 despite Hakimi red card Kylian Mbappé stayed calm to roll home a 100th-minute penalty and secure for Real Madrid a 2-1 win over nine-man Rayo Vallecano on Sunday in a spicy La Liga derby. Los Blancos’ victory cut Barcelona’s lead back to one point at the top of the table after the Spanish champions beat Elche on Saturday. Vinícius Júnior scored early on for Madrid after Jude Bellingham limped off with an apparent hamstring injury. Jorge de Frutos pulled Rayo level early in the second half, prompting visible anger from the Madrid fans at their team. Frustration was averted when Mbappé netted from the spot following Pathé Ciss’s red card at the death. Rayo also had Pep Chavarría sent off before the final whistle. Continue reading...

items.title: Mourinho lijkt af te haken in Portugese titelstrijd na nieuw puntenverlies
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zondag 1 februari. Hier namen we je mee in drie Eredivisie-duels, een topper in Engeland en alle andere hoogtepunten op de (inter)nationale velden.

items.title: 'NEC is de gedoodverfde favoriet voor de tweede plek'
items.summary: PSV is op weg naar de landstitel in de Eredivisie, maar de strijd daaronder ligt helemaal open. Feyenoord en Ajax verloren opnieuw punten dit weekend, waardoor NEC minder verliespunten heeft dan de twee topclubs. Marciano Vink ziet NEC zelfs als favoriet voor de plek achter PSV.

items.title: Foord sees off Corinthians in extra time to put Arsenal on top of the world
items.summary: Final: Arsenal 3-2 Corinthians (aet) Smith 15, Wubben-Moy 58, Foord 104; Zanotti 21, Albuquerque 90+6 pen The most decorated women’s club in England made more history at the Emirates stadium on Sunday night, Arsenal securing a 3-2 win over the Copa Libertadores champions Corinthians in extra time to see them crowned winners of the inaugural Fifa Women’s Champions Cup. They were made to work for their victory, the Brazilians twice coming from behind to force another 30 minutes of football, but it was somewhat of an inevitability. The Champions League winners benefited from being mid-season with players at full fitness, in contrast to Corinthians being in their pre-season and Concacaf Champions Cup winners Gotham FC in their off-season, and from the decision to hold the tournament in London, and play the final at the Emirates. This was a competition set up for European success and Arsenal delivered. They are officially the world’s best club and they have a nice trophy to prove it. The 13-point gap, albeit with a game in hand, between them and WSL leaders Manchester City though, says otherwise. Continue reading...

items.title: Guardiola complains over Solanke’s first goal after Manchester City blow 2-0 lead
items.summary: ‘If it’s a central defender to a striker it’s a penalty, right?’ Thomas Frank says Spurs ‘going in the right direction’ Pep Guardiola was left frustrated once again by a refereeing decision as Manchester City lost ground in the Premier League title race after a ­chaotic 2-2 draw at Tottenham. The City manager complained that the Spurs goal for 2-1, which sparked an outlandish second-half comeback, should not have stood. Dominic Solanke kicked through the back of the City defender Marc Guéhi, the ball then going in but neither the referee nor the VAR felt there was enough in it for a foul to be given. Continue reading...

items.title: Hits and misses: Man City get just rewards for sloppy second halves
Solanke scorpion kick helps Spurs fight back against Man City
items.summary: Dominic Solanke scores twice, including a spectacular scorpion kick, as Tottenham come from behind to draw against Manchester City with a rousing second-half performance.

items.title: Sesko scores dramatic late winner as Man Utd beat Fulham
items.summary: Manchester United avoided Fulham's late comeback due to a Benjamin Sesko goal in stoppage time to give United three points and continue head coach Michael Carrick's perfect start.

items.title: Sarr earns Palace draw at Forest as Williams sees red for handball
items.summary: Neco Williams is sent off for deliberate handball in Nottingham Forest's draw with Crystal Palace, as he punched Jefferson Lerma's goalbound header off the line.

items.title: 10-man Brentford hold on for hard-fought win at Villa
items.summary: Brentford hold on to an important win against Aston Villa, despite Kevin Schade's sending off during the first half.
